_notes.neuropeptides: No neuropeptide data found in any of the 5 targeted papers — gap confirmed open
key_findings: Schmidt-Hieber 2004: immature granule cells have T-type Ca2+ channels; LTP threshold lower than mature cells
Esposito 2005: silent(1-7dpi) → slow GABA(8-18dpi) → glutamate(≥18dpi) → fast GABA; EGABA=-26.9mV in immature neurons; mirrors embryonic sequence; mouse
Ge 2006: tonic GABA from 3dpi; GABAergic PSCs at 7dpi; glutamatergic PSCs at 14dpi; NKCC1-high/KCC2-low in DCX+ neurons; NKCC1 knockdown impairs dendritic arborisation; mouse
Tozuka 2005: type-2 progenitors receive direct GABAergic (not glutamatergic) input; GABA depolarises due to high [Cl-]i; drives Ca2+ and NeuroD; nestin-GFP mouse
van Praag 2002: first patch-clamp proof of functional integration; Vth=-43mV; plateau ~140Hz; Cm 42.3pF (vs 99.2 mature); integrated by 4 weeks; mouse

themes_identified: Electrophysiological maturation: silent -> GABA -> glutamate -> fast GABA sequence
GABA is depolarising and excitatory in neuroblasts and immature granule neurons
Enhanced excitability and lowered LTP threshold in immature granule neurons
Marker progression and stage identity across the neuroblast -> immature -> mature axis
